skill.name: clarity-gate
skill.description: Pre-ingestion verification guidance for improving epistemic quality in RAG systems using a 9-point review and two-round human workflow.
skill.summary: Review RAG source material before ingestion with structured quality checks and human review rounds.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static network findings are hardcoded GitHub source links in SKILL.md, not executable network behavior. The static weak cryptography findings are false positives on descriptive text; no cryptographic code, commands, prompt injection, or data exfiltration patterns were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Source Repository Links
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged hardcoded URLs at SKILL.md lines 4 and 22. These are GitHub source references in metadata and documentation, with no fetch, callback, tracking, or data transmission behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines contain plain GitHub repository links. The skill has no executable code or instruction to send user data over the network.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at SKILL.md lines 3 and 20. The lines only describe pre-ingestion verification for RAG systems and do not contain cryptographic algorithms or security-sensitive hashing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are natural-language descriptions. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, or similar weak cryptographic implementation appears in the reviewed file.

content.value_statement: RAG pipelines can absorb weak, stale, or unsupported source material before anyone checks it. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured source verification before ingestion.
actual_capabilities: Frames source review before content enters a RAG knowledge base.
Applies a 9-point verification concept to assess source quality.
Supports a two-round human review workflow for higher confidence.
Helps identify unclear, unsupported, or low-quality material.
Provides reusable guidance for teams preparing retrieval content.
limitations: Does not include scripts, validators, or automated ingestion tooling.
Does not verify facts without access to source documents and references.
Does not replace expert review for regulated or high-risk domains.
Does not define a full RAG architecture or retrieval evaluation suite.

use_cases.title: Prepare Enterprise Knowledge Sources
use_cases.description: Review policy, support, and product documents before adding them to an internal RAG system.

use_cases.title: Audit Dataset Readiness
use_cases.description: Check whether document collections are clear, sourced, and suitable for retrieval workflows.

use_cases.title: Run Human Review Rounds
use_cases.description: Coordinate two rounds of human judgment before approving documents for ingestion.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Source Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one document before ingestion.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use clarity-gate to review this document for RAG ingestion readiness. Identify unclear claims, missing support, and quality concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Knowledge Base Intake Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Screen a small batch of source material.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the clarity-gate approach to these source documents. Summarize which items are ready, which need revision, and why.

prompt_templates.title: Two-Round Review Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare human review steps for a content approval workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a two-round human review workflow for these RAG ingestion candidates. Include reviewer roles, decision criteria, and escalation triggers.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Epistemic Quality Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use clarity-gate as an epistemic quality framework. Evaluate source reliability, claim support, ambiguity, conflicts, and ingestion risk.

output_examples.input: A support article contains product limits but no publication date or owner.
output_examples.output: The article needs revision before ingestion. Add an owner, publication date, source of limits, and a review deadline.

output_examples.input: A policy document has clear approvals but contains conflicting retention periods.
output_examples.output: The document should enter a second review round. Resolve the conflict before it becomes retrievable guidance.

output_examples.input: A technical guide cites official release notes and matches current product behavior.
output_examples.output: The guide appears suitable for ingestion after routine metadata checks and reviewer sign-off.

best_practices: Review source documents before chunking, embedding, or indexing them.
Record reviewer decisions and reasons for every rejected source.
Use a second human review for high-impact or ambiguous material.
anti_patterns: Do not ingest documents only because they are easy to parse.
Do not treat missing ownership, dates, or citations as minor issues.
Do not use the skill as a substitute for domain expert review.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps review source material before it enters a RAG knowledge base.

faq.question: Does it run automated checks?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill file provides guidance and patterns, not executable tooling.

faq.question: Can it improve retrieval quality?
faq.answer: It can improve inputs by identifying weak sources before ingestion.

faq.question: Who should use it?
faq.answer: Data teams, AI governance teams, and knowledge managers can use it during intake review.

faq.question: Does it require external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found in the skill file that it requires external services.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no confirmed security issues in the reviewed file.
